# Transcrição: 03- 3 Ways to Be Funny

- **Fonte:** 03- 3 Ways to Be Funny
- **Duração:** 07:10
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:04]

It's time to be funny. Or at least it's time to learn to be funny. My name is Matthew Dix. I'm a storyteller, a stand-up comic, a novelist, a writer, many other things, but today I'm going to teach you how to be funny. The good news is I can teach you to be funny. Humor is a skill that can be learned. I can't promise you that after you learn the strategies contained in this course, you will go into the world and be a naturally funny person. You're not going to be able to sit down in a restaurant and just spontaneously be funny. That may come, but what I can teach you to do is I can teach you how to be funny in a prepared way. In other words, I can teach you the strategies that you can use to craft talks and stories and even stand up comedy into something that's funny. I can help you prepare to be funny. And then over time, as you begin using these strategies, I think they'll start to sink in. I think they'll find their way into your bones and then eventually you will become a funnier person. And that's great because when you make people laugh, amazing things happen. When you cause someone to laugh, their brain releases three chemicals and those chemicals make them feel good, improve their cognition, and make them feel better about the world. And then the miraculous thing about that is the brain then attributes that good feeling to the source of the comedy, which will be you. In other words, when you make people laugh, you're a more likable, attractive, and trusted human being. So there's great value in becoming a funnier person. Here's the great news. There's only three ways to make people laugh. Now I'm going to take those three ways and I'm going to break them into five strategies that you will learn in this course. And I have 25 strategies so you can buy more of my courses and learn more of my strategies. But all of it boils down to three ways, three ways that we make people laugh in our everyday lives, three ways that stand up comedians and storytellers and filmmakers, all of these people use to make people laugh. I'm going to teach you the three right now. The first one and the one that is used almost always is surprise. A laugh is really nothing but a collection of words that strike your brain in such a way that produce a laugh. It's a way of assembling language in a way that causes you to be surprised in a humorous way. I'm going to teach you strategies that will help you assemble words in such a way that produce a laugh. But almost all laughter is the result of surprise. The second way you can make people laugh is through anticipation. Anticipation happens when the audience, whether it's one person or a thousand people, they can foresee the future

## [03:05 - 06:09]

and they will begin laughing at what they know the future will bring. For example, I tell a story about a time when I was the stripper for a bachelorette party in the crew room of a McDonald's restaurant. The moment I say yes and I agree to be that stripper, the audience laughs. Nothing funny has actually happened yet. They're laughing because they understand what that decision will mean for me in the future. They can see the future and begin laughing already. One of my heroes, Mike Brabiglia, he has a great line he uses in all of his shows when the audience begins laughing in anticipation. He says, I know I'm living in the future with you. That is anticipation. That is, I've said something that isn't funny, but the future is objectively funny and that produces a laugh. The third way you can make people laugh is through empathy. They will laugh if they see your situation and they can see themselves in your situation as well. This is when you tell stories and jokes that resonate with the audience in such a way that they think, I have also been in that situation too. I tell a story where I'm walking into the grocery store and I see the Boy Scouts parked outside the grocery store selling candy bars, which are the last things I want to buy at that moment. The story describes how I dodge the Boy Scouts, how I avoid giving them money for their candy bars because I want nothing to do with them. As soon as the audience sees those Boy Scouts and I talk about not wanting to give them my money, they begin laughing. They're laughing in empathy. They're laughing with the recognition that I too have been through that circumstance. I have walked into the grocery store and been accosted by small children who want my money and I can't stand it. And so, for that reason, they laugh. Those are the three reasons. That's the only three reasons that people laugh in this world. Surprise, which is about 90% of the time. Anticipation, they see the future, and they laugh at what's coming and empathy. They feel you, they understand you, they have been in that circumstance, they have laughed at themselves, and now they're laughing at you too. That's it. Three ways to make people laugh. Now, that I recognize is not very helpful. That did not really teach you how to craft humor, but that is sort of the overarching philosophy that we have to understand. The strategies that I'm about to teach you, the strategies I'm going to offer, they're going to be more specific. But here's what I want you to understand. With all of my strategies, everything is everything, which is to say, you may look at strategy number two and say, wait a minute, strategy number two is essentially strategy number four, just a different way of looking at it. Correct. What I'm going to be providing you is a variety of lenses to be looking at humor, different ways to see surprise, anticipation, and empathy in the work that you do. Because if I

## [06:09 - 07:10]

tell you, just go surprise people, that doesn't help you at all. But if I tell you, here's one way to surprise people, and here's another way to surprise people, and here's our third way to surprise people, you'll quickly recognize they're all very related. In fact, they're almost the same, they're not. They're different ways of looking at the same thing, different lenses that will expand your understanding, your ability, your capacity to be funny. So please remember, everything is everything. I don't want you to email me and tell me that strategies two and five are essentially the same thing. I agree, they're essentially the same thing. My goal is to get you to see the world in a variety of ways to expand the opportunities you have to be funny. In the end, there's three ways, but lots and lots and lots of strategies to find those three ways in the world and become a funnier person. Let's go.
